Command & Control.name: COM_I_EN_REG
Command & Control.description: Control bits to enable and disable the passing of interrupt requests

fields.name: IRQINV
fields.description: Signal on pin IRQ is inverted with respect to the Status1Reg register's IRq bit

fields.name: TXI_EN
fields.description: Allows the transmitter interrupt request to be propagated to pin IRQ

fields.name: RXI_EN
fields.description: Allows the receiver interrupt request to be propagated to pin IRQ

fields.name: IDLEI_EN
fields.description: Allows the idle interrupt request to be propagated to pin IRQ

fields.name: HIALERTI_EN
fields.description: Allows the high alert interrupt request to be propagated to pin IRQ

fields.name: LOALERTI_EN
fields.description: Allows the low alert interrupt request to be propagated to pin IRQ

fields.name: ERRI_EN
fields.description: Allows the error interrupt request to be propagated to pin IRQ

fields.name: TIMERI_EN
fields.description: Allows the timer interrupt request to be propagated to pin IRQ

fields.name: RSVD_6_0
fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: Set1
fields.description: Indicates that the marked bits in the ComIrqReg register are cleared (0) or set (1).

fields.description: Reserved

fields.name: SET1
fields.description: Indicates that the marked bits in the ComIrqReg register are cleared

fields.name: MFCRYPTO1ON
fields.description: MIFARE Crypto1 unit on

fields.name: MODEMSTATE
fields.description: Transmitter/receiver state

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: Reserved for future use.

fields.name: TSTOPNOW
fields.description: Timer stops immediately; reading this bit always returns 0.

fields.name: TSTARTNOW
fields.description: Timer starts immediately; reading this bit always returns 0.

fields.name: RXLASTBITS
fields.description: Indicates the number of valid bits in the last received byte.

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: STARTSEND
fields.description: starts the transmission of data only valid in combination with the Transceive command

fields.name: RXALIGN
fields.description: used for reception of bit-oriented frames: defines the bit position for the first bit received to be stored in the FIFO buffer

fields.name: TXLASTBITS
fields.description: used for transmission of bit oriented frames: defines the number of bits of the last byte that will be transmitted 000b indicates that all bits of the last byte will be transmitted

fields.name: RESERVED
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: VALUESAFTERCOLL
fields.description: all received bits will be cleared after a collision only used during bitwise anticollision at 106 kBd, otherwise it is set to logic 1

fields.name: COLLPOSNOTVALID
fields.description: no collision detected or the position of the collision is out of the range of CollPos[4:0]

fields.name: COLLPOS
fields.description: shows the bit position of the first detected collision in a received frame only data bits are interpreted

fields.name: ModWidth

Timer.name: TModeReg

fields.name: TAuto
fields.description: timer starts automatically at the end of the transmission in all communication modes at all speeds; if the RxModeReg register’s RxMultiple bit is not set, the timer stops immediately after receiving the 5th bit (1 start bit, 4 data bits); if the RxMultiple bit is set to logic 1 the timer never stops, in which case the timer can be stopped by setting the ControlReg register’s TStopNow bit to logic 1; 0 indicates that the timer is not influenced by the protocol

fields.name: TGated[1:0]
fields.description: internal timer is running in gated mode; Remark: in gated mode, the Status1Reg register’s TRunning bit is logic 1 when the timer is enabled by the TModeReg register’s TGated[1:0] bits; this bit does not influence the gating signal

fields.name: TAutoRestart
fields.description: timer automatically restarts its count-down from the 16-bit timer reload value instead of counting down to zero; 0 timer decrements to 0 and the ComIrqReg register’s TimerIRq bit is set to logic 1

fields.name: TPrescaler_Hi[3:0]
fields.description: defines the higher 4 bits of the TPrescaler value

Modulation.name: GsNReg

fields.name: CWGsN
fields.description: defines the conductance of the output n-driver during periods without modulation which can be used to regulate the output power and subsequently current consumption and operating distance. Remark: the conductance value is binary-weighted during soft Power-down mode the highest bit is forced to logic 1 value is only used if driver TX1 or TX2 is switched on.

fields.name: ModGsN
fields.description: defines the conductance of the output n-driver during periods without modulation which can be used to regulate the modulation index. Remark: the conductance value is binary weighted during soft Power-down mode the highest bit is forced to logic 1 value is only used if driver TX1 or TX2 is switched on.

quality_issues: missing_register_page: source pages exist but logical register page is unknown

Modulation.name: CWGsPReg

fields.name: reserved
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: CWGsP[5:0]
fields.description: defines the conductance of the p-driver output which can be used to regulate the output power and subsequently current consumption and operating distance. The conductance value is binary weighted. During soft Power-down mode the highest bit is forced to logic 1.

quality_issues: missing_register_page: source pages exist but logical register page is unknown

Modulation.name: ModGsPReg

fields.name: reserved
fields.description: reserved for future use

fields.name: ModGsP[5:0]
fields.description: defines the conductance of the p-driver output during modulation which can be used to regulate the modulation index. Remark: the conductance value is binary weighted. During soft Power-down mode the highest bit is forced to logic 1. If the TxASKReg register’s Force100ASK bit is set to logic 1 the value of ModGsP has no effect.
